channel.title: Conference | Ian Buller, PhD, MA
channel.copyright: © Ian Buller, 2026 · Opinions expressed are my own

item.title: American Geophysical Union 2025 Annual Meeting
item.description:
I presented a poster at the American Geophysical Union 2025 Annual Meeting entitled “Temporal clustering of air pollution and heat wave events at domestic U.S. Military facilities.”

I presented initial results comparing three statistical approaches (supervised and unsupervised machine learning approaches) to evaluate the potential relationship between heat and air pollution wave events at U.S. Military Facilities within 4 km of U.S. Environmental Protection Agency Air Quality System sampling locations. We also used publicly-available PRISM Climate Group data from Oregon State University with our custom-built open-source software.

item.title: Military Health System Research Symposium 2025
item.description:
I presented a poster at the Military Health System Research Symposium 2025 entitled “Open-source estimation of shade availability at military bases in the United States.”

I presented an initial workflow of estimating the shadow footprint (shade) of obstacles on United States (US) Military facilities using the US Naval Academy campus as an example. We use publicly-available US Geological Survey 3D Elevation Program point cloud data with custom-built open-source software.

item.title: American Geophysical Union 2024
item.description:
I presented a lightning talk at the American Geophysical Union Annual Meeting 2024 entitled “Ambient PM2.5 exposure and risk of non-Hodgkin lymphoma in a large U.S.-based cohort.”

I presented initial results from a project conducted by Inam Ghulamhussain who I mentored while I was a postdoctoral Cancer Prevention Fellow and he was a National Institutes of Health Summer Intern at the National Cancer Institute.

item.title: Society for Epidemiologic Research 2024
item.description:
I co-instructed a workshop at the Society for Epidemiologic Research Annual Meeting 2024 entitled “Git’n up to speed on versioning control.”

The workshop was an introduction to the Git language and other Git-enabled technology for epidemiologists to version control their software code. I developed the course with other DLHers, including Dr. Ghassan Hamra, Dr. Nat MacNell, and Audrey Brown.

I was also a co-author on a poster abstract entitled “Revisiting the modifiable areal unit problem in the era of exposome-wide association studies: Assessing the performance of the CDC/ATSDR Social Vulnerability Index at privacy-protecting spatial scales” led by Jonathan Lewis.

item.title: 14th AACR Conference on The Science of Cancer Health Disparities in Racial/Ethnic Minorities and the Medically Underserved
item.description:
I was a co-author on a poster entitled “Comparing the association of self-reported race-ethnicity and genetic ancestry with all-cause mortality: A pan-cancer survivor analysis in the PLCO Screening Trial” at the 14th AACR Conference on The Science of Cancer Health Disparities in Racial/Ethnic Minorities and the Medically Underserved by Dr. Derek Brown and Dr. Jacqueline Vo held virtually due to the global COVID-19 pandemic.

This effort was funded by our 2021 William G. Coleman Minority Health and Health Disparities Research Innovation Award.

item.title: 2021 FDA-NIH-NIST-USDA Joint Agency Microbiome (JAM) Symposium
item.description: I presented a poster entitled “Geographic variation in the oral microbiome of NIH-AARP Diet and Health Study participants” at the 2021 FDA-NIH-NIST-USDA Joint Agency Microbiome (JAM) Symposium held virtually due to the global COVID-19 pandemic. This poster was previously presented at the International Society for Environmental Epidemiology 2020 Annual meeting.

item.title: International Society for Environmental Epidemiology 2021
item.description:
I presented my oral abstract entitled “Roadway Proximity and Lung Cancer Risk in NIH-AARP Diet and Health Study Participants” at the 33rd Annual Conference of the International Society for Environmental Epidemiology held virtually due to the global COVID-19 pandemic.

I was also a co-author on three abstracts:

  1. “Residential proximity to animal feeding operations and mortality among postmenopausal women in the Iowa Women’s Health Study” led by Dr. Jessica Madrigal
  2. “Residential proximity to animal feeding operations and risk of lymphohematopoietic cancers in the Iowa Women’s Health Study” led by Dr. Jared Fisher
  3. “Ethylene oxide emissions and risk of breast cancer and Non-Hodgkin lymphoma in a large U.S. cohort” led by Dr. Rena Jones

item.title: Society for Epidemiologic Research 2021
item.description: I presented my invited talk entitled “Estimating environmental mixtures in a geospatial context” in a symposium entitled “Implementing geolocation-based exposure assessments” at the Society for Epidemiologic Reserach 2021 annual meeting held virtually due to the global COVID-19 pandemic.

item.title: Society for Epidemiologic Research 2020
item.description: I presented my abstract entitled “Geographic variation in the oral microbiome of Agricultural Health Study applicators” at the Society for Epidemiologic Reserach 2020 annual meeting held virtually due to the global COVID-19 pandemic.

item.title: International Society for Environmental Epidemiology 2020
item.description:
I presented my abstract entitled “Geographic variation in the oral microbiome of NIH-AARP Diet and Health Study Participants” at the 32nd Annual Conference of the International Society for Environmental Epidemiology held virtually due to the global COVID-19 pandemic.

I was also a co-author on an abstract entitled “Ingestion of nitrate and nitrite from drinking water and diet and cancers of the gastrointestinal tract in Iowa women” led by Dr. Mary Ward.
